claim: Context rot (performance degradation from large context) causes 80% of Claude Code failures - not the model itself but context management
actionable: When AI coding fails, first audit your context management strategy (WISC framework) before blaming the model
source_title: I've Used Claude Code for 2,000+ Hours - Here's How I Build Anything With It

claim: Larger context windows increase LLM susceptibility to distractor tokens (similar but wrong information) via needle-in-haystack problem
actionable: Keep context minimal even with large token limits to reduce distractor interference in LLM reasoning
source_title: I've Used Claude Code for 2,000+ Hours - Here's How I Build Anything With It

claim: WISC framework (Write, Isolate, Select, Compress) systematically manages agent context to maximize coding reliability
actionable: Apply WISC framework: externalize memory via git logs, isolate research via sub-agents, select context just-in-time, compress only when necessary
source_title: I've Used Claude Code for 2,000+ Hours - Here's How I Build Anything With It

claim: Using Git commit messages as long-term agent memory reduces context rot by offloading decision history to version control
actionable: Standardize Git commit messages to include implementation details and agent improvements, then reference git log in planning sessions
source_title: I've Used Claude Code for 2,000+ Hours - Here's How I Build Anything With It

claim: Always plan in one context window and implement in a fresh context window, passing the plan as the only initial context
actionable: Separate planning from implementation into different Claude Code sessions to keep implementation context lean and focused
source_title: I've Used Claude Code for 2,000+ Hours - Here's How I Build Anything With It

claim: Sub-agents for research reduce main context window by 90%+ by summarizing research findings instead of loading raw research
actionable: Spin up sub-agents to explore codebases and do web research, then load only their summaries into main context
source_title: I've Used Claude Code for 2,000+ Hours - Here's How I Build Anything With It

claim: Scout pattern uses sub-agents to determine relevance before loading documentation into main context, avoiding unnecessary context bloat
actionable: Have sub-agents pre-evaluate which documentation is relevant before loading it into your main coding context
source_title: I've Used Claude Code for 2,000+ Hours - Here's How I Build Anything Without It

claim: Layered context selection: global rules (always load), on-demand rules (for specific tasks), skills (load on-demand), prime commands (explore live codebase)
actionable: Structure context in layers: core rules always loaded, task-specific rules on-demand, capabilities as skills, and dynamic exploration via prime commands
source_title: I've Used Claude Code for 2,000+ Hours - Here's How I Build Anything With It

claim: The /handoff and /compact commands in Claude Code enable resuming work in fresh sessions without context rot from long conversations
actionable: Use /handoff to summarize conversation before starting fresh session, or /compact once when nearing context limits
source_title: I've Used Claude Code for 2,000+ Hours - Here's How I Build Anything With It
